stories.headline: GOP senator who voted to convict Trump in impeachment trial loses primary

stories.summary: Russia’s capital and the surrounding Moscow region came under record drone attacks overnight, leaving at least three people killed and over a dozen wounded.

stories.headline: WHO Declares Congo Ebola Outbreak Global Health Emergency
stories.summary: The World Health Organization declared an Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, warning that a rare virus strain with no approved vaccine or treatment may already be spreading more widely than detected.

stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, a two-term Republican who voted to convict President Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial,  took a veiled swipe at Mr. Trump, saying power should not focus on “one individual.”

stories.summary: Bill Cassidy is among seven Republican senators who voted to convict Trump after the January 6, 2021, Capitol attack.

stories.headline: New Book Details Silicon Valley's Grip on College Campuses
stories.summary: Author Theo Baker joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura this morning on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss his new book "How to Rule the World: An Education in Power at Stanford University," chronicling the prize-winning reporting he did for Stanford Univeristy's student newspaper -- reporting that led to the resignation of the university's president. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Emerging Carry Trade Rebounds With Real, Rand Among Favorites
stories.summary: The emerging-market carry trade has bounced back from its Iran war losses as surging crude oil prices reinforce expectations that interest rates will stay elevated and bolster the currencies of commodity exporters.

stories.headline: Malaysia’s Coalition Tensions Deepen as Anwar Floats Snap Poll
stories.summary: Fresh strains are emerging in Malaysia’s ruling alliance as political jockeying and oneupmanship among the coalition’s parties led Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim to raise the prospect of a snap general election if divisions deepen.

stories.headline: Philippines Asks Court to Deny Petition of Senator Sought by ICC
stories.summary: The Philippine government asked the Supreme Court to deny a petition by Senator Ronald Dela Rosa to stop his arrest after the International Criminal Court issued a warrant on allegations of crimes against humanity.

stories.headline: Can New York City Tax the Rich Without Driving Them Away?

stories.headline: Tech Bubble Fear Lures Investors to Hedge With Exotic Options
stories.summary: The tech-fueled stock rally is looking bubble-like to some investors, and they’re turning to exotic options that better protect against an eventual slump.

stories.headline: Trump Ethics Filings Reveal Unusually High Tech Stock Trades
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s latest financial disclosures show that he or his investment advisers made more than 3,700 trades in the first quarter, a flurry totaling tens of millions of dollars and involving major companies that have dealings with his administration. Bloomberg News Senior Equities Reporter Jess Menton joined joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura this morning on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Volatile Chipmaker Stocks Emerge as Key Driver of S&P 500 Rally
